criteria_verification.criterion_text: All seven Protocol classes are concrete and pass `isinstance` checks
criteria_verification.evidence: Implemented seven Sqlite*/AsyncTask* classes (SqliteBuildSnapshotReader, SqliteBuildCanceller, SqliteBuildResumer, SqliteStageSkipRecorder, SqlitePauseRejectResolver, AsyncTaskCanceller, AsyncTaskUpdater) in src/forge/lifecycle/persistence.py. Each class exposes the methods declared on its corresponding runtime_checkable Protocol from forge.pipeline.cli_steering. Verified via TestProtocolImplementations (7 tests, each calling isinstance against the Protocol) — all pass. Confirmed at runtime by manual REPL check showing isinstance() returns True for all seven Sqlite*/AsyncTask* classes.
criteria_verification.notes: Implemented seven Sqlite*/AsyncTask* classes (SqliteBuildSnapshotReader, SqliteBuildCanceller, SqliteBuildResumer, SqliteStageSkipRecorder, SqlitePauseRejectResolver, AsyncTaskCanceller, AsyncTaskUpdater) in src/forge/lifecycle/persistence.py. Each class exposes the methods declared on its corresponding runtime_checkable Protocol from forge.pipeline.cli_steering. Verified via TestProtocolImplementations (7 tests, each calling isinstance against the Protocol) — all pass. Confirmed at runtime by manual REPL check showing isinstance() returns True for all seven Sqlite*/AsyncTask* classes.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `apply_transition(t: Transition) -> None` is the ONLY public method
criteria_verification.evidence: The single 'UPDATE builds SET status = ?' SQL is inside SqliteLifecyclePersistence.apply_transition. Other state-changing methods (mark_paused, SqliteBuildCanceller.mark_cancelled, SqliteBuildResumer.resume_after_skip) compose a Transition via state_machine.transition() and route it through apply_transition rather than emitting their own UPDATE. Verified by TestSingleStatusWriter.test_single_status_writer_in_src which walks src/ for the regex and asserts exactly one hit, located in lifecycle/persistence.py. Also verified by the existing test_state_machine.py grep-based static-analysis test (still passing after a small fix to add --include=*.py to skip .pyc cache).
criteria_verification.notes: The single 'UPDATE builds SET status = ?' SQL is inside SqliteLifecyclePersistence.apply_transition. Other state-changing methods (mark_paused, SqliteBuildCanceller.mark_cancelled, SqliteBuildResumer.resume_after_skip) compose a Transition via state_machine.transition() and route it through apply_transition rather than emitting their own UPDATE. Verified by TestSingleStatusWriter.test_single_status_writer_in_src which walks src/ for the regex and asserts exactly one hit, located in lifecycle/persistence.py. Also verified by the existing test_state_machine.py grep-based static-analysis test (still passing after a small fix to add --include=*.py to skip .pyc cache).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `apply_transition` rejects raw kwargs — its signature accepts only
criteria_verification.evidence: apply_transition(self, transition: Transition) -> None has exactly one parameter named 'transition'. Body raises TypeError if isinstance(transition, Transition) is False. Verified by TestApplyTransitionSignature.test_signature_has_only_transition_param (introspects via inspect.signature) and test_apply_transition_rejects_non_transition (asserts TypeError for dict, str, None inputs).
criteria_verification.notes: apply_transition(self, transition: Transition) -> None has exactly one parameter named 'transition'. Body raises TypeError if isinstance(transition, Transition) is False. Verified by TestApplyTransitionSignature.test_signature_has_only_transition_param (introspects via inspect.signature) and test_apply_transition_rejects_non_transition (asserts TypeError for dict, str, None inputs).

criteria_verification.evidence: mark_paused composes a Transition with pending_approval_request_id=request_id via state_machine.transition() and routes it through apply_transition. apply_transition's UPDATE statement writes both 'status = ?' and 'pending_approval_request_id = ?' in the same statement (single SQL UPDATE), guarded by BEGIN IMMEDIATE/COMMIT. Verified by TestMarkPausedAtomic.test_mark_paused_persists_request_id which checks both columns after the call.
criteria_verification.notes: mark_paused composes a Transition with pending_approval_request_id=request_id via state_machine.transition() and routes it through apply_transition. apply_transition's UPDATE statement writes both 'status = ?' and 'pending_approval_request_id = ?' in the same statement (single SQL UPDATE), guarded by BEGIN IMMEDIATE/COMMIT. Verified by TestMarkPausedAtomic.test_mark_paused_persists_request_id which checks both columns after the call.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `record_pending_build(payload)` writes the build row and translates
criteria_verification.evidence: record_pending_build wraps the INSERT in BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT and converts sqlite3.IntegrityError to DuplicateBuildError(feature_id, correlation_id) with ROLLBACK on the failed transaction. Verified by TestRecordPendingBuildDuplicate which seeds two builds with the same (feature_id, correlation_id), asserts DuplicateBuildError is raised, and confirms only one row exists in the table after the failed second insert.
criteria_verification.notes: record_pending_build wraps the INSERT in BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT and converts sqlite3.IntegrityError to DuplicateBuildError(feature_id, correlation_id) with ROLLBACK on the failed transaction. Verified by TestRecordPendingBuildDuplicate which seeds two builds with the same (feature_id, correlation_id), asserts DuplicateBuildError is raised, and confirms only one row exists in the table after the failed second insert.

criteria_verification.evidence: read_status issues two SELECTs (active set and terminal LIMIT 5), concatenates and sorts the result by queued_at DESC. Verified by TestReadStatus with four cases: empty db, active-only ordering, terminal cap at five (seeded 7 cancelled, asserted 5 returned), and combined active+terminal global DESC sort.
criteria_verification.notes: read_status issues two SELECTs (active set and terminal LIMIT 5), concatenates and sorts the result by queued_at DESC. Verified by TestReadStatus with four cases: empty db, active-only ordering, terminal cap at five (seeded 7 cancelled, asserted 5 returned), and combined active+terminal global DESC sort.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `read_history(limit=50)` returns at most 50 most recent builds; clamps
criteria_verification.evidence: read_history clamps limit via max(0, min(limit, MAX_HISTORY_LIMIT)) where MAX_HISTORY_LIMIT=1000. Default is 50. Negative or zero limit returns []; non-int raises TypeError. Verified by TestReadHistory with four cases: default 50 (60 builds seeded → 50 returned newest-first), clamp to MAX, zero/negative, type guard.
criteria_verification.notes: read_history clamps limit via max(0, min(limit, MAX_HISTORY_LIMIT)) where MAX_HISTORY_LIMIT=1000. Default is 50. Verified by TestReadHistory with four cases: default 50 (60 builds seeded → 50 returned newest-first), clamp to MAX, zero/negative, type guard.

criteria_verification.evidence: exists_active_build issues SELECT 1 ... WHERE feature_id = ? AND status IN (...) LIMIT 1 against the ACTIVE_STATES frozenset (QUEUED, PREPARING, RUNNING, PAUSED, FINALISING). Verified by TestExistsActiveBuild — empty case False, QUEUED True, three terminal states (parametrized COMPLETE/FAILED/CANCELLED) False, PAUSED True.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: All write paths use `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` to acquire the write lock
criteria_verification.evidence: apply_transition, record_pending_build, and record_stage all open transactions with explicit 'BEGIN IMMEDIATE;' followed by COMMIT/ROLLBACK on the autocommit (isolation_level=None) writer connection. Verified by TestBeginImmediate.test_persistence_module_uses_begin_immediate which scans the module text for the literal 'BEGIN IMMEDIATE'. The mark_paused and Sqlite*Canceller/Resumer paths inherit this guarantee because they delegate to apply_transition.
criteria_verification.notes: apply_transition, record_pending_build, and record_stage all open transactions with explicit 'BEGIN IMMEDIATE;' followed by COMMIT/ROLLBACK on the autocommit (isolation_level=None) writer connection. The mark_paused and Sqlite*Canceller/Resumer paths inherit this guarantee because they delegate to apply_transition.

criteria_verification.evidence: SqliteLifecyclePersistence._reader() context manager opens a fresh read_only_connect() against self._db_path on every read call (read_status, read_history, read_stages, exists_active_build, find_active_or_recent). For in-memory test databases (path is empty) it transparently falls back to the writer connection — file-backed production paths always use the ro helper. Verified by TestReadPathsUseReadOnlyConnect.test_reads_against_real_file_open_a_ro_connection which monkeypatches read_only_connect with a spy and asserts every read API exercises it.
criteria_verification.notes: SqliteLifecyclePersistence._reader() context manager opens a fresh read_only_connect() against self._db_path on every read call (read_status, read_history, read_stages, exists_active_build, find_active_or_recent). For in-memory test databases (path is empty) it transparently falls back to the writer connection — file-backed production paths always use the ro helper. Verified by TestReadPathsUseReadOnlyConnect.test_reads_against_real_file_open_a_ro_connection which monkeypatches read_only_connect with a spy and asserts every read API exercises it.

- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)
